name: Seated Mini-Band Shoulder + Upper-Back Flow
description: 53-second seated flow with one mini-band (looped, light-medium tension). Heels-tucked seiza/half-lotus base. Continuous flow through ~7 banded shoulder positions — overhead pull-aparts, behind-neck pulldowns, lateral T-reaches, chest pull-aparts, diagonal reaches. No reps counted in the video — appears to be ~5-8s sustained tension at each position. Excellent banded scap/upper-back mobility primer; very 'old-school yoga teacher with a band' energy. Could slot into the W11+ morning flow rotation, especially before pull or push days.
extractionNotes: Video is silent flow (no spoken cues, no rep counts) — exercise breakdown is reconstructed from frames at 1fps over 53s.
Mini-band (loop band) used throughout, never swapped. Looks like medium-resistance.
Athlete is in heels-tucked kneeling/seiza posture the entire video — no standing.
Each position held ~5-8s with sustained tension. No counted reps.
Exercises 4-5 are the most useful 'stretches' the user flagged — the lateral T-reach and chest-level pull-apart open up a lot of upper back.
All exercises here are CANDIDATES — none yet exist in exercises/. Would need new exercise files before this workout can be built into the catalogue.

sets.name: Continuous Flow (~5-8s per position, no rest)

exercises.name: Seated banded overhead pull-apart
exercises.notes: Sit heels-tucked. Band looped between hands, arms straight overhead, hands wider than shoulders. Pull band apart to create tension, ribs down, shoulders away from ears. Sustained tension, slow micro-pulses.

exercises.name: Seated banded behind-neck pulldown
exercises.notes: From overhead position, pull the band down behind the head/neck — elbows drive down toward the ribs, shoulder blades retract. Like a band lat-pulldown without the cable.

exercises.name: Seated banded T-raise (lateral reach)
exercises.notes: Band wraps around the upper back/shoulder blades, hands hold the band ends in front. Press arms straight out to the sides into a T — band creates resistance against the rear delts and mid-traps. Palms can rotate forward.

exercises.name: Seated banded chest pull-apart
exercises.notes: Band held in front at sternum height, arms straight. Pull band apart, scaps retract, chest opens. Slower than the overhead version — feels like the 'core' opener of the sequence.

exercises.name: Seated banded reverse fly
exercises.notes: From the chest pull-apart position, sweep arms back further behind the body line. Rear delts and rhomboids drive it. Subtle — easy to overrotate the lower back here.

exercises.name: Seated banded diagonal reach (high-low triangle)
exercises.notes: One arm reaches high overhead, other reaches diagonal-out — band makes a slanted line/triangle. Like a half-windmill with band. Switches sides in the video. Lat + side-body opener.

exercises.name: Seated banded overhead V-hold
exercises.notes: Closer to a Y-hold — both arms reach high and wide, band stretched between hands in a V/diamond. Hold the tension at end-range, breathe. Closes the flow.

tips: All seven moves use one mini-band, looped — no equipment swap.
Seated heels-tucked posture forces upper-back work without lower-body cheat.
Sustained tension, NOT reps. Hold each position ~5-8s with steady pull, breathe.
Best slot: morning flow primer before push or pull day. Hits the same scap-prep work as wall slides + face pulls but with less setup.
If shoulder/neck tight, drop band tension before reducing range of motion.
